title: agrostis alba subsp. dubia
intro: Agrostis alba subsp. dubia, also known as a type of grass or a subspecies of the white bent grass, is a plant species that belongs to the Poaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues and as an anti-inflammatory agent. The plant is rich in fiber, vitamins, and minerals, and is believed to have health benefits such as regulating bowel movements, reducing inflammation, and improving overall digestive health. Agrostis alba subsp. dubia can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is often used in traditional medicine to support gut health and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome.
